programme.title: The Postcard Killings
programme.desc: A New York detective investigates the death of his daughter, who was murdered while on her honeymoon in London, the victim of an apparent serial killer.

programme.title: True History of the Kelly Gang
programme.desc: An exploration of Australian bushranger Ned Kelly and his gang as they attempt to evade authorities during the 1870s.

programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: Welcome to the Punch
programme.desc: When a notorious criminal is forced to return to London, it gives a veteran detective one final chance to bring the man to justice.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: Rage
programme.desc: Following the kidnapping and murder of his daughter (Aubrey Peeples), a reformed criminal (Nicolas Cage) returns to his old ways to exact vengeance.

programme.title: A Score to Settle
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a fatal condition, a mob enforcer leaves prison after 19 years and plots a bloody course of revenge against his old gang.

programme.title: Dog Eat Dog
programme.desc: Three ex-cons (Nicolas Cage, Willem Dafoe, Christopher Matthew Cook) find themselves on the run from the mob and the police after botching a plan to kidnap a baby for a large ransom.

programme.title: Nitram
programme.desc: Living a life of isolation and frustration, a young man develops an unexpected friendship with a reclusive heiress. When that relationship meets its tragic end, his loneliness and anger culminates into the most nihilistic and heinous of acts.

programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: White Elephant
programme.desc: When two cops witness an assassination attempt, a ruthless crime boss orders Gabriel Tancredi, an ex-Marine turned mob enforcer, to eliminate any and all threats.

programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Gangubai Kathiawadi
programme.desc: A young woman, sold to a brothel, rises from victim to ruler by leveraging her underworld connections, turning the world that once controlled her into her own domain of power.

programme.title: Annapoorani: The Goddess Of Food
programme.desc: Annapoorani, A Girl Born Into An Orthodox Hindu Family, Aspires To Become A Top Chef. However, She Faces Obstacles That Include Prejudice And Conventional Beliefs.

programme.title: Khatta Meetha
programme.desc: A petty contractor sues a city over withheld pay as a bridge collapse tragedy unfolds, while his ex-girlfriend-turned-commissioner blacklists him amid legal and personal chaos.

programme.title: Wanted

programme.title: Shaadi No. 1
programme.desc: Raj, Veer and Aryan, three men perpetually ignored by their wives, accept an offer from a businessman to seduce and betray his daughters so that they become more open to an arranged marriage.

programme.title: Tarla
programme.desc: Tarla celebrates the legendary home chef, Tarla Dalal, who empowered women through cooking in the 70s-80s. Her inspiring journey from kitchen to nationwide fame resonates with women today.

programme.title: Turtle
programme.desc: While Ramkaran is forced to churn water from the earth in a drought-struck village, the plight of villagers intensifies as they seek different means to quench their thirst.

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of a fiercely independent Hollywood star who left it all behind to live life on her own terms, revealing a haunting, resilient journey of art, identity and authenticity in the shadow of one of cinema’s most iconic roles.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boards the West Highland Line to begin a railway journey across Scotland’s central belt, from the Arrochar Alps to the Loch of the Lowes. At the head of Loch Long, Michael meets a legendary octogenarian munro-bagger. In Balloch, on the shores of Loch Lomond, he joins passionate volunteers working to restore an iconic 1950s paddle steamer. In Glasgow, Michael rides the 'Clockwork Orange', the third oldest underground rail system in the world, to Govan and the shipyards of the River Clyde. And at Kelvinbridge, Michael hears how the chicken tikka masala was created for the Glaswegian palate by an immigrant chef from Lahore.

programme.desc: Bob Harris introduces Linda Ronstadt in concert at the New Victoria Theatre, London, in 1976.

programme.title: Linda Ronstadt: The Sound of My Voice
programme.desc: Musical biography of Linda Ronstadt, one of the most successful and versatile female singers of the 20th century - and one of the most successful recording artists of all time. It tells Linda's story through her own words and music, and by such professional colleagues as Dolly Parton, Emmylou Harris, Bonnie Raitt, Jackson Browne, Aaron Neville and many more.

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with distinct takes on country, but how they ended up uniting as close harmony singers on Trio.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 January 2000 and featuring Britney Spears, Mint Royale feat Lauren Laverne, Lyte Funky Ones, Andreas Johnson, DJ Luck & MC Neat, REM, Daphne & Celeste and Gabrielle.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 11 February 2000 and featuring Andreas Johnson, Fierce, Sisqó, The Tamperer feat Maya, Sash!, Britney Spears, Oasis and Gabrielle.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Simon Bates present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 16 July 1987 and featuring Bananarama, Bruce Willis, Hue and Cry, Kenny G, Mel and Kim, Los Lobos, The Cure, Boogie Box High, Freddie McGregor, Pet Shop Boys and Madonna.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 July 1990 and featuring Paul Young, Inspiral Carpets and Dream Warriors.

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with distinct takes on country, but how they ended up uniting as close harmony singers on Trio.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The touching story of a rescued giant otter cub being raised by a Brazilian naturalist.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Fish occur in populations of billions and there are over 30,000 species, more than in any other group of backboned animals. The development of the backbone was a crucial advance in evolution - and it probably came from a most unlikely source, a little jelly-like creature called a sea squirt.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose tells the story of his hero Fridjtof Nansen who, in 1892, announced a daring plan to be first to the North Pole, an idea considered so off-the-wall that no scientist would volunteer to join him on a venture they believed was nothing short of suicide. He allowed his ship to become stuck in the crushing pack ice, hoping it would drift to the Pole, and then set off on foot across the frozen wastes. Nansen became the forefather of polar exploration, inventing practical techniques that today allow people to survive, travel and work in the most hostile and forbidding places on our planet.

programme.title: The Sentinels
programme.desc: Gabriel’s wife, Irène, decides to conduct her own investigation into her husband’s alleged death. Captured by French forces, German spy Gruber is interrogated regarding the rival program. While out on a mission, the Sentinels learn of the feats of a German soldier with formidable powers – the Übermensch. In French and German with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Sentinels
programme.desc: The army’s General Staff orders the elimination of the Germans' superhuman, now the Sentinels’ primary enemy. The Sentinels head off on his trail, unaware that they are about to face a foe with powers beyond all measure. In French and German with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Black Forest Murders
programme.desc: A hair found in the Schora case leads the team to a suspect, but there is still no breakthrough in the Berghoff case. Navit's relentless data searches yield a surprising result – the suspect is implicated in the unsolved murder of a French student, across the border in Austria, four years previously. In German with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Black Forest Murders
programme.desc: Progress is slow as the investigation reaches the 100-day mark. As Kramer prepares to downsize the team, they finally manage to glean evidence from the murder weapon in Steinach, narrowing the search to potential perpetrators. In German with English subtitles.

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Stately sitcom. When DeVere decides to film a commercial at the manor, Audrey thinks the world should know the truth about him - and about her.

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom finds marketing his garden produce more difficult than he thought, especially when the rates are due.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Fish occur in populations of billions and there are over 30,000 species, more than in any other group of backboned animals. The development of the backbone was a crucial advance in evolution - and it probably came from a most unlikely source, a little jelly-like creature called a sea squirt.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: If you think Britain has recently been on the end of some of the worst floods and storms ever experienced, think again. So says solar scientist Dr Lucie Green, as she takes a journey back through our most turbulent and dramatic weather history. She finds an 18th-century storm surge that killed over a thousand people working in open Somerset fields, a hurricane that drowned a fifth of the British Navy and winters so bitter that the country came close to total shutdown. But she also explores how our reactions to killer storms and cruel winters helped forge a weather science that today allows us to predict - and protect ourselves from - the worst extremes.

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham introduces a classic documentary from the BBC's archive, which takes a look at the worst winter of the 20th century in 1963. He also explores what we now know about how this big freeze affected Britain's wildlife, and how it would cope if we experienced another equally bad winter.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A specially curated night of musical gems, from the Royal Albert Hall. Alongside Beethoven's famous symphony are Mussorgsky's A Night on a Bare Mountain and Strauss' Don Juan. Presented by Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann and featuring performances by the BBC Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Marin Alsop and Elim Chan.

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: The A303 is the road that passes Stonehenge on the way to the beaches of Devon and Cornwall. On the way, it whisks drivers through 5,000 years of remarkable moments in British history. And it is the star of this film made for armchair travellers and history lovers. Writer Tom Fort drives its 92-mile length in a lovingly restored Morris Traveller. Along the way, he has many adventures - he digs up the 1960s master plan for the A303's dreams of superhighway status, and meets up with a Neolithic traveller who knew the road like the back of his hand. He also gets to know a section of the Roman 303, uncovers a medieval murder mystery and discovers what lies at the end of the Highway to the Sun.

programme.title: Hans Zimmer: Hollywood Rebel
programme.desc: The story of one of the most celebrated and sought-after movie composers, as told by the man himself and his closest collaborators. Across a glittering 40-year career, Hans has redefined the movie score, thrilling audiences, pioneering new techniques and introducing new generations to the drama of orchestral music. In this portrait, Hans reveals the musical secrets of his craft - how he goes about terrifying, moving and raising an audience's spirits through his music.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A specially curated night of musical gems, from the Royal Albert Hall. Alongside Beethoven's famous symphony are Mussorgsky's A Night on a Bare Mountain and Strauss' Don Juan. Presented by Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann and featuring performances by the BBC Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Marin Alsop and Elim Chan.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Nigel Farage has accused parts of the British media of mounting a politically-motivated campaign against him after reports about his financial interests prompted an investigation by Parliament's Standards Commissioner. Gabriel Pogrund, editor of the Insight investigations team at The Sunday Times, outlines the reporting behind their story, while journalist Michael Crick and Brendan O'Neill of Spiked discuss the political and journalistic issues. A High Court judgment has dismissed all claims brought by Prince Harry and six other claimants against Associated Newspapers over allegations that journalists used unlawful methods to obtain information. The ruling marks a significant moment in the long-running legal and political legacy of Britain's phone-hacking and press intrusion scandals. Susan Aslan, media lawyer, outlines the legal significance of the judgment.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Traditional Chinese gardens represent every aspect of the natural landscape. They are an inherent part of people's lives, both physically and spiritually. Alastair Sooke travels from north to south, to examine ancient paintings and visits surviving historic gardens. His aim is to understand how Chinese aesthetics and philosophy are reflected in the design of traditional gardens.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: World's Ultimate Adventures
programme.desc: Cultural historian Janina Ramirez has long been fascinated by the legend of Troy, immortalised in the epic works of Homer. The tales of a ten-year battle, the beauty of Queen Helen and the trick of the Trojan horse that brought an ancient civilisation to its knees has inspired storytellers from Shakespeare to Christopher Nolan, from Chaucer to Margaret Atwood. Up until 150 years ago, many believed that the ancient city was a mythological place, but on this journey, Janina travels to north western Turkey to explore its ancient ruins. In the Museum of Troy, the glitter of gold will catch her eye as she gets up close with some of treasures that have been unearthed from the site. She crosses the glistening Aegean Sea to the island of Bozcaada, where she'll learn how to hunt the waters for a local seafood delicacy before making the journey north to the Gallipoli Peninsula. Here, she'll walk the beaches where Allied troops landed in 1915 and explore the World War I trenches that remain. Janina is astounded by the beauty of the region, and it is the layers of buried human history that will stay with her.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: The Trump administration has issued subpoenas to several New York Times journalists following reporting about alleged security concerns involving President Trump's new Air Force One, a plane donated by Qatar. The case has prompted debate over the balance between protecting national security and safeguarding press freedom. Claire Atkinson, founder of The Media Mix newsletter, outlines the latest developments. The BBC's latest annual report comes as the corporation warns that public service media is facing a period of jeopardy. The figures show a further fall in licence fee-paying households, alongside changing audience habits and continued financial pressures as charter renewal negotiations continue. Rhodri Talfan Davies, the BBC's deputy director-general, sets out the corporation's position. Broadcaster and author Iain Dale reflects on a career spanning politics, publishing, blogging and radio following the publication of his memoir Have I Said Too Much?. The interview covers the enduring appeal of the radio phone-in, the influence of social media on broadcasting, and changing relationships between politicians and traditional media.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: This year, the Finnish city of Oulu is European Capital of Culture. The Nordic country is consistently ranked as the happiest place on earth, despite shivering in sub-zero temperatures for many months of the year. So could its culture be the reason why? Rob Bells heads to Oulu to see how warmly people there embrace the snow and ice, travels deep into Lapland to see how children visiting Santa Claus have transformed the region and sees for himself the deep bond between the Finns and their reindeer.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI can write, code, plan and problem-solve in seconds. But as we hand more tasks over to machines, what happens to our own skills? In this episode, AI Decoded investigates whether AI is enhancing human intelligence or quietly eroding it. From doctors and software engineers to students growing up with AI tutors, we explore new research suggesting that reliance on AI could affect critical thinking, expertise and decision-making. Joining Marc are leading paediatric neurosurgeon Owase Jeelani, digital transformation researcher Dr Tapani Rinta-Kahila and AI Decoded co-host Dr Stephanie Hare.

This week, Shawn Yuan is in Mexico investigating the extraordinary story of one of the world's most wanted fugitives - a man accused of fuelling the global fentanyl crisis. And from Mexico to Kenya, BBC Eye goes inside the online manosphere.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Hidden away above the bat cave, conservationists are investigating a worrying decline in one of the park's rare native bat colonies. Numbers have fallen sharply, and there are fears another species may be threatening the next generation. As the team search for answers, they hope to discover whether the colony can recover before more bats are lost. As part of the show's silver anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate revisit one of the most memorable stories from the past 25 years. As the anniversary celebrations continue, Ben and Kate look back at the time a binturong mysteriously started refusing food, seriously worrying the keepers. The only option is an anaesthetic to try to get to the root of the problem, but with any procedure of this kind there is always a worry that the animal may never wake up. Elsewhere, the keepers celebrate an important milestone as a critically endangered white-backed vulture chick reaches a crucial stage in its development. Megan McCubbin also joins the keepers to weigh the park's three male gorillas. With each weighing more than twice as much as the average adult man, it's no easy task getting them onto the scales.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A family home is targeted in an arson attack – and two people die. But the matches police found are damp and only give a partial DNA profile. There is nothing to put the main suspects at the scene until an eagle-eyed detective realises there is something wrong with the CCTV footage. Also, police hunting for a burglar who’s stolen hundreds of thousands of pounds' worth of jewellery get their moment of proof from a distinctive piece of clothing.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic biologist uses modern DNA techniques to solve the cold case murder of Hilda Murrell, and cell site analysis helps place a suspect at the scene of Abdul Deghayes’s killing.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a pair of cottages in north Norfolk and plan to create one stunning family home. Martel meets a developer in North Yorkshire, who puts his DIY skills to the test refurbishing a three-bedroom semi-detached house. In Somerset, Martin tours a tired terrace that has just five weeks to be transformed for new tenants.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine visits Hereford, a city closely linked to the military, to see a collection of Second World War vehicles, equipment and memorabilia. At The Bunker, she meets Colin and Kathryn Taylor, World War II enthusiasts and custodians of this living museum, to learn about the heroic work of the military police, field medics and the famous SAS. From the passenger seat of a restored Jeep and a Humber ambulance, Roo sees how Colin and Kathryn are helping to keep history alive. Over in Leominster, two teams of bargain hunters are on manoeuvres, seeking profitable antiques to take to auction. On team red are twin sisters Jo and Sarah with their expert Charles Hanson. On the blue team are father and daughter Bobby and Jas, with Richard Madley. With £300 in their pockets, Bobby and Jas spot a desktop barometer and thermometer set and decide it’s a fair-weather option for later. Across the antiques centre, Charles think he's seeing double when Jo picks up an art nouveau sculpture that he believes looks just like her! Up in Wrexham, auctioneer Matthew Tong casts a keen eye on the teams’ items before putting them under the hammer.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Hairdresser Mysteries
programme.desc: Hairdresser Lily Petal opens her new salon in the sleepy village of Blossom Vale, finding herself investigating a suspicious death and the theft of a valuable antique.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick travels to Australia's famous holiday hotspot Byron Bay. On his way, he takes a detour to the fishing town of Ballina, just south of the Byron Bay hinterland.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home after eight years of living and working in Abu Dhabi, two teachers want to swap desert sand for the beaches of East Sussex, where Briony May Williams has the keys to what could be the couple’s new home. Keen to get involved in the local community, they visit the Bluebell Railway to see what volunteering opportunities there are on the heritage line.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and Lauren help Emma downsize a home full of memories after losing her husband. Finding items full of stories in his home library, the family will turn his loss into legacy.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Quick hands, sharp instincts and a custom-built race car. Follow Scotland's first wheelchair-using racing driver as he prepares for his debut season.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark puts Lauren to the test, Vicki and Ross face another stumbling block, and Gina attempts to right a wrong.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: The team help the Clarke family, whose home has been overwhelmed by the hobbies they love, leaving little room for everyday life.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guest panellists Harry Hill, Kojey Radical, Jill Scott and Stacey Solomon.

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Sue’s husband Dave died two years ago, and now her only son, Michael, is moving out – again – to try to fix his marriage – again. But the good news is he has got Sue a surprise to help her live independently: an Ann Droid Z58/100 humanoid care robot. Sue really does not want a robot in her home, especially one that causes chaos straightaway. But is this robot actually the companion she most needs?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: Austin misreads a dinner as a date, Julian battles a book title, and Ingrid debates betraying their motto 'No pair, no bear'.

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: Austin battles cultural branding, Ingrid wrangles with her new star, and Julian accidentally talks their Big Bear actor into quitting.

programme.title: My Old Ass
programme.desc: In this coming-of-age fable, free-spirited teenager Elliott encounters her wisecracking 39-year-old future self and can’t escape her influence. On the cusp of leaving home for college, Elliott is determined to have one last summer packed with good times. To celebrate her 18th birthday, she boats off to a remote island with her best friends to trip on magic mushrooms. But just when Elliott thinks she’s not feeling a thing, she suddenly finds herself chatting with a woman who claims to be her literal 'old ass', the person she is apparently destined to become in two decades' time! Prodded for advice, Elliott’s future self gives her one major warning: avoid anyone named Chad, which sounds easy enough. But when Elliott meets the very Chad she was supposed to dodge, things get complicated. Elliott starts to open herself to what her future self might have to show her... and vice versa!

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Poppy O'Toole and Paul Ainsworth, plus special guests Jordan North and William Hanson. Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Presenter Angela Scanlon invites Anna to explore the rugged landscape of County Mayo on Ireland’s Atlantic coast. Anna uncovers the resourcefulness of Achill Island's salt producers before visiting Angela’s favourite pub in Belmullet for potato boxty. No trip to Ireland's west coast would be complete without celebrating the amazing seafood on offer, so Angela and Anna hop on a speedboat to harvest mussels out on the scenic Clew Bay.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the teams hunt for antiques in Cambridgeshire with experts Colin Young and Raj Bisram. Who will come out on top at the auction in Norfolk? Eric takes some time out to visit the local Norris Museum to look at a collection of ice skates and learn about the golden age of fenland skating.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Today’s buyers don’t mind if they find a new home in either Hampshire or Dorset, just as long as it’s as close as possible to their happy place, the sea. Denise Nurse is there to put a smile on their faces, and she also meets a Hampshire family who are living the Escape to the Country dream in an idyllic Georgian gatehouse.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Mike and Val have always enjoyed their garden, but now Val isn’t able to access it, so it's time to call in Garden Rescue. The couple would love a fragrant rose garden, and Chris and Charlie are the designers battling to win them over. The couple go for Chris' design, and the first task on the build is to create a seamless new deck from the back of the house to the upper level of the garden, which is quite a feat of engineering. Chris’s design is for a more traditional rose garden, with the central feature being a circular brick and sandstone patio under a stunning octagonal pergola. Meanwhile, Charlie wants to help Rose to start gardening again and so comes up with a design for a multi-use potting table. But the real stars are the roses, and Chris has included every variety in his planting plan to give Val the garden she wants.

programme.title: Alan Carr's Picture Slam
programme.desc: Comedian Alan Carr returns to host the fun and frantic quiz show where every question is a picture and every picture is worth cash. They could be anything from famous faces, places, products or logos, and teams need to name them all for a Picture Slam. Playing this time are friends Selin and Harin from London, cousins Gerard and Jeremy from South Wales and Kev and Bex, a couple from Motherwell. They battle it out across four rounds, shouting out answers and building their banks. But only one team can make it to the final Super Slam for the chance to win £10,000.

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman present a special celebrity edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. The celebrities competing for the Pointless trophy are Gavin Ramjaun, Sally Magnusson, Pete Wicks, Vicky Pattison, Jay Rayner, Thomasina Miers OBE, Remel London and London Hughes.

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts one of the world’s most iconic quizzes, where eight celebrities are all hoping to avoid The Walk of Shame. They must work together to bank as much money as possible for charity, before voting off one fellow contestant per round as the weakest link. Only one player will be crowned the strongest link and take home the prize. Celebrity contestants in this episode are Joe Pasquale, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Charlotte Crosby, Tracy-Ann Oberman, Sandro Farmhouse, Helen McGinn, Russell Watson and Beth Tweddle.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: Join us for the peaceful and spiritually uplifting Isha prayers from the Belfast Islamic Centre, which serves as a hub for Muslims across Northern Ireland. The prayers are the final of the five daily prayers in Islam, and take place after sunset, providing an opportunity for worshippers to reflect on their day and look ahead to the future.

programme.title: The Addams Family 2
programme.desc: Wednesday is delighted to find a like-minded genius, who happens to be the sponsor of her school's science fair. When an irritating lawyer arrives at the family home, Gomez takes the Addams on an impromptu and diverting trip around America, but Wednesday is haunted by doubt. Why is she so different from the rest of her family?

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in the historic city of Hull to explore how music, culture and faith are bringing new life to this historic city, resulting in it being named one of the top places in the world to visit in 2026. Aled goes to Hull Minster and learns from musical director Mark Keith how its choir was saved ten years ago and is now thriving, partly due to an innovative partnership that takes music into local primary schools to spread the joy of singing. Aled also visits the workshop of Helen Richardson, who transforms old or discarded items into treasure, all inspired by God’s restorative powers. And Aled joins a remarkable local church that meets in its own adventure park, complete with high rope courses and zip wires! He hears from Minister Jarrod Cooper how the facilities help promote fun, family and a sense of community. With glorious music, including some specially recorded hymns from a packed Hull Minster: 1. Hark, My Soul, It Is yhe Lord – Hull Minster 2. A Gaelic Blessing – Hull Minster 3. Holy Forever – Way Church, Wigan 4. O Thou Who Camest from Above – Hull Minster 5. King of Kings, Majesty – The Mint Methodist Church, Exeter 6. How Great Thou Art – Hull Minster

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley’s buyer today is chasing an inspiring landscape. She wants a new Carmarthenshire home that has as much land as her £450,000 budget can muster. She’s also keen to make some green-fingered, like-minded friends in the area, so a trip to meet some volunteers at the National Garden of Wales is also on the agenda.

programme.title: The Mating Game
programme.desc: The strategies used by animals to find and defend their mates in the grasslands of Namibia, the Serengeti and Australia. Here, mating strategies are on show for all to see.

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Revealed as the web-slinging superhero, Peter Parker wants the world to forget, so he begs a spell from Dr Strange, but with grave consequences. Foes from across the multiverse materialise – along with an unexpected friend or two. Blockbuster Marvel action movie.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita continues her east coast road trip, visiting the Naze, a nature reserve on the Essex coast. Here, coastal erosion is particularly severe, with around 2 to 3 metres lost from the cliffs every year. But there are benefits to a crumbling coastline – it can reveal 55 million years of history. Anita joins a group of enthusiastic fossil hunters on the lookout for remnants of the coast's fascinating past. She also discovers what has been done to protect the area’s heritage and visits one of over a thousand brightly coloured beach huts overlooking the North Sea. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the hidden histories found across our landscape.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
programme.desc: A vengeful anomaly on Miles Morales’s Earth that Gwen Stacy is sent to stop means the parted teens reunite – but destructive consequences affect the multiple Spider-being universes from which Miles has been isolated. Animated superhero action comedy sequel.

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: A body falls from the fourth storey of a construction site. Was it just a terrible accident or a perfectly executed murder? John’s team think he’s losing his touch when he latches on to a far-fetched theory and starts pointing fingers. When things don’t quite add up, John begins to question his abilities both at work and at home, whilst Lucy’s frustration at their lack of progress in finding her husband reaches fever pitch.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds out that a big house on her street is owned by an old lady who is planning to move out. So, she inserts herself into her life, hoping to get first refusal on the sale.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda tries to persuade Felicity to give her the money she needs to buy her dream house. Meanwhile, Fi buys some wheels for her business, which transforms her into a white-van man.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda hosts a prom pre-party at her flat for Georgie and her friends, but Felicity gets in the way, the make-up designer is terrible, and the limo gets stuck.

programme.title: Pretty Woman
programme.desc: Multimillionaire Edward Lewis gets lost in Los Angeles during a business trip. After he pays Hollywood Boulevard prostitute Vivian to direct him to his hotel, the pair form an unlikely partnership as he hires her to stay with him, but they discover there are significant hurdles to overcome as they try to bridge the gap between their two very different worlds.

programme.title: The Kim Kardashian Diamond Heist
programme.desc: Who was behind the infamous gunpoint robbery of Kim Kardashian? Those close to the case reveal details about the crime for the first time.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Lara’s vigil, Miri races back to Mandy’s house to get ready for her date with Billy, the first proper date she has ever had. But Mark intercepts her and pleads with her to find Mandy. With the discovery of John at the lighthouse, it becomes clear that the road to Miri’s first date will be a long and winding one. Meanwhile, in the Matteson house, Caroline and Oscar’s bickering is interrupted by the doorbell. It’s Dom, and fearing what will happen if they leave him on their doorstep, the couple let him in.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Making the strongest coffee in the world won’t help Miri’s nerves about Mandy and what has happened to John. Her stress levels are exacerbated when Janice reminds Miri that her driving test is today. Caroline and Oscar wake to find an unnervingly chirpy Dom making them breakfast. And when it comes time for Dom to leave, it’s clear he doesn’t want to, so Oscar reluctantly ropes him in to help with Make Hythe Clean. Miri waits for her driving test examiner and almost brings herself to tell Caroline what happened the night before. But when her examiner arrives Miri must put all of that, and the whereabouts of her treasured digital pet, out of her mind.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: With John’s fate discovered, no sign of her digital pet and no word from Mandy, Miri realises that she has to go to the police before they come to her. This is probably her last morning of freedom, and she wants to spend it with Billy. They will have fun together, even if an unexpected family member comes along for the ride. Oscar and Caroline are united after their strange night with Dom, and they set off on a litter-picking mission for Make Hythe Clean. But will Caroline’s love for Oscar trump her fear of humiliation and ridicule, and allow her the freedom to be herself? Meanwhile, Norah leaves a surprise behind for Miri before beating her to the police station, where Tina is investigating John’s disappearance.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Zygons, a race of shapeshifting aliens, have been living in secret amongst us on Earth, unknown and unseen - until now! When Osgood is kidnapped by a rogue gang of Zygons, the Doctor, Clara and Unit must scatter across the world in a bid to set her free. programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: Shapeshifting Zygons are everywhere in the UK, and there is no way of knowing who to trust. With UNIT neutralised, only the Doctor stands in their way. But how do you stop a war? And what can the Doctor do to save his friends?

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee organises a family camping trip in an attempt to bring the generations together. But even though he has remembered to bring his guitar for campfire sing-songs, he's forgotten to check the weather. And when Lucy finds something in the woods that looks very much like a human bone, the unhappy campers cannot wait to leave.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee does jury service and joins 11 other jurors to debate whether the accused is guilty or not. A simple enough task - until Lee decides to put the other jurors on trial.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that her son Trevor has been called back to the missions in Africa, but she is determined to give him a massive send-off to remind him what a loving family he has back home in Ireland. Meanwhile, daughter Cathy's family counselling session descends into chaos, and Buster and Dermot run into trouble with a circus lion. The only person having a good day is neighbour Winnie: thanks to his new medication, her husband Jackos's libido is back. But while Winnie delights in reminiscing about her former bedroom antics, Agnes is shocked to discover that orgasms actually exist!

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Baby fever hits the Brown household as a pregnant Maria prepares for motherhood, and Agnes is keen to give her new daughter-in-law her unique brand of advice about raising children. Maria is grateful when Agnes offers to look after the robot baby she has brought home from her prenatal class, but it turns out that Mrs Brown's babysitting techniques are not exactly orthodox. And Maria has some exciting news for the Brown family.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken forces Richard to accompany him to Aviemore so that he can help him navigate the QR code scanner at the train station ticket barrier. However, a mix-up on the platform turns what should be a simple day trip into an almighty fiasco.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken is looking forward to having Beth over for dinner, but not before he has painted over the damp patch on the living room ceiling. Richard just wants to read his book in peace, but when Emily and Tim also turn up, an unexpected party gets under way. As the night progresses, secrets threaten to spill out.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: The diggers move in at the allotments, forcing the protest group to take extreme action. With time running out for Richard, he decides to lay it all on the line for Emily - but will it prove enough?

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: It’s been several days since the sale of the town fell through, and the Roses are still searching for their missing son David. It soon becomes clear, however, that Moira may be even more worried about the bag that David took with him. Meanwhile, Alexis must make a difficult choice between Ted and Mutt. Luckily, she’s able to delay her decision when David is found in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: In the hope of showcasing her culinary prowess, Moira decides to make dinner for the family and enlists David’s help. Johnny searches for office space in order to start a new business. Ted continues to press Alexis for a response to his marriage proposal.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira finds out that Jocelyn is the leader of the town’s a cappella group, the Jazzagals, and expects to join. David plans to build a cedar box for his expensive sweaters and gets some unlikely help from Mutt. Johnny discovers that working out of Bob’s Garage requires him to be a little more hands-on than he’d hoped.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick regrets showing off to his new number two, Towerblock. Simon and Bird come to blows over a special book belonging to the lieutenant colonel.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: A blast from the past knocks Nick off his game. Bird tries to teach Mary a lesson that the padre is out of touch with the troops.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick and the lieutenant colonel find themselves in an unexpected battle of wills. Rocket gets himself a pet rat, much to Bird's disgust.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animation. It’s 1995, and welcome to Dave’s Games: your one-stop shop for all things video games, from computers and consoles to poppers and Pogs - and how can you forget the floor-to-ceiling piles of plastic game cartridges? Self-appointed ‘gaming legend’, shop owner and father (in that order), Dave is the king of his own crumbling kingdom. Sure, there is mould on the ceiling and some of the oldest video games you’ve ever seen (even for 1995), but it’s the only games shop in town. At least until the gigantic, shiny, futuristic rival shop Turbo Gamez opens just down the road. And they also happen to be releasing the newest and most sophisticated piece of gaming technology the world has ever seen: the first PlayStation…

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: On a Bradford estate, Lenny and Mullet find an abandoned car and turn it into a DIY spaceship. A kitchen-sink science fiction comedy about estate childhoods, big dreams and escaping without going anywhere.

programme.desc: Ian battles with a decision, Max makes an offer, and the Fox-Truemans prepare to welcome Denise home.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise struggles to settle back into family life, it’s the day of Ian’s plea hearing, and Mark raises suspicions.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise lays the law down, Vicki and Ross return from their honeymoon, and Mark’s doubts start to grow.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark puts Lauren to the test, Vicki and Ross face another stumbling block, and Gina attempts to right a wrong.

programme.title: The Notebook
programme.desc: In the run-up to the Second World War, a young couple's relationship falls foul of class prejudice.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Best friends Evie and Noa have watched films together since they first met at university. Eight years on, Evie isn’t coping with life anymore, so she’s moved back home to live with her mum, Suz, and sister Izzie in the Manchester suburbs. Film Club’s moved with Evie – to Suz’s garage. Unable to face the world outside and trapped at home with little else to occupy her, Evie pours all her creativity into decorating the space beautifully to reflect the movie they’re watching every week and making herself the perfect costume. But a change on the horizon is bringing up newfound feelings for Evie and Noa. Or feelings they’ve never admitted to themselves or each other…

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Noa attends a taster day for his new job in Bristol, Evie pulls out all the stops for Film Club – there’s a lot riding on tonight.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evie’s still reeling from the message she saw on Noa’s phone. But when the two are left alone together, something extraordinary happens. Is it the start of a new journey for Evie?

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Evie throws herself headlong at life, Suz and Josh worry it’s too much, too soon. Meanwhile, Noa realises he has to face up to his feelings...

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Alan watches on as crewmates Tracey and Lauren help his wife Jean from the floor of their caravan. Alarm bells ring as checks reveal she is suffering from life-threatening sepsis.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie’s juggling college and family life, learning about teenage mental health, online pressures and autism - and how care reaches far beyond the classroom.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: After many happy years of married life, Cheryl’s beloved husband Pete passed away just a few months ago. A great collector and skilled with his hands, Pete was affectionately known by his family as Mr Fixit. But sadly, after surviving a stroke at a young age, Pete went on to suffer several years of poor health, and many of his ambitious projects remain incomplete. Leaving behind a number of partly restored motor vehicles, as well as an overwhelming amount of potentially valuable toys and novelty items, it’s time for a fresh start. But for Cheryl, who also lost her mum last year, the task of clearing out is more than she can take right now. So, she’s asked auctioneer Izzie Balmer and restorer Robin Johnson to help her. It’s transport galore for Izzie and Robin as they uncover a Velocette bicycle, a tricycle, a boat and even a scratch-build 4x4. Robin turns his specialist skills to an early e-bike, while Izzie hunts for treasures inside. At auction, a Royal Albert County Rose tea, coffee and dinner service wows the crowds, selling for over £600, and all the money raised mounts up to be shared among Pete’s family. A fitting tribute to Mr Fixit.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben and Kate celebrate 25 years of friendship, animal stories and unforgettable moments on Animal Park, and revisit the extraordinary life of lioness Malaika.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus wants to impress the locals with a French version of the classic Sunday roast - but first, he needs the perfect poulet. He also gives a masterclass on how to portion a whole chicken and tries to find the best wine to accompany the dish. Later, Marcus finds out how the French enjoy their take on the sandwich. Baguette in hand, he hits the market stalls looking for inspiration.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the grand final of the classic quiz where six contenders return to the black chair in a bid to become the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are British and Irish Lions tours in the 21st century, Princess Margaret, the Rocky films, Bletchley Park during the Second World War, Augustus Caesar and the BBC sitcom Extras.

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farming couple Tim and Tanya have breathed new life into their Cotswold farm, ensuring that Tim’s young son has the chance to take on a viable business in the future. Their most ambitious venture was three years in the making: they have recently launched the UK’s first sheep’s milk vodka. However, at £60 per bottle, the challenge will be to sell it. Tim and Tanya have high hopes that they can reel in potential buyers for their artisan spirit at Blenheim Palace Food Festival, even before lunchtime! Meanwhile, Wye Valley-based brothers Matt and Kit are working round the clock collecting honey from 150 beehives and making it into sparkling mead. As a young family business, they rely on their own teamwork to make the dream work. They hope bottles will fly off their stall once people sample their modern take on this ancient drink. On top of that, creating a buzz at this prestigious event could bring them closer to landing a lucrative deal.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom serves up more revelations from the world of hospitality. This time, his attention turns to street food, which is experiencing something of a boom when the industry is struggling. He meets the founders of a street food collective that brings togeter local traders with DJs and live music; an authentic Thai food outfit taking the leap to bricks and mortar; and a barbecue smokehouse in Cornwall that resolutely stays open all year round. And in Marlow, it’s the opening night of Tom’s own food festival, Pub in the Park.

programme.title: Land of the Lost Wolves
programme.desc: After spending four weeks camping out in the bitterly cold Cascade Mountains, wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan and a team of wolf experts discover that most of the pack have been killed by locals who live by the motto 'shoot, shovel and shut up'. But have any of the pack escaped unharmed? As the team chase exciting new leads, there is a glimmer of hope on the horizon.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from the RAF Museum at Hendon in London. Antiques experts Michael Baggott and Mark Stacey find some real gems among the bags and boxes to take to auction. There's a nautilus shell that lights up the saleroom and some Superman film memorabilia that goes sky high. And Paul Martin explores the history of one of Britain's most famous tourist attractions; Highgate cemetery.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Ever wonder if doom-scrolling is draining your joy? You’re not alone. The science says cutting smartphone use to just two hours a day could seriously boost your wellbeing – but actually doing it is another story. So Zoe Ball is in Kettering to share with busy beautician Lucie some clever digital detox tricks that could improve family life, reduce stress and even boost her brain power. Less scrolling, more living.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: The world’s greatest classical music festival gets under way live from the Royal Albert Hall, with works by Copland, Gershwin and Ravel. Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann guide us through a night of spectacular music-making to kick off the summer's Proms season. Copland’s landmark Fanfare for the Common Man opens, followed by Gershwin’s ever-popular An American in Paris, and Ravel’s bluesy Piano Concerto, performed by piano superstar Yunchan Lim. A world premiere by Josephine Stephenson starts the second half, followed by Finzi’s ode to the patron saint of music – For St Cecilia – with tenor Thomas Atkins. Dalia Stasevska conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra, BBC Singers and BBC Symphony Chorus.

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Highlights from the second day of the 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. World number one Scottie Scheffler defends the title he won in emphatically in 2025, and world number two Rory McIlroy looks to add a second Claret Jug to the second Green Jacket he won at the Masters in April. Southport local Tommy Fleetwood will have the support of the home crowd as he aims to land his first major title, fellow Englishman Matt Fitzpatrick hopes to cap a stellar season by becoming the Champion Golfer of the Year, while Justin Rose returns to the venue where he famously burst onto the scene back in 1998.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Insomnia
programme.desc: Psychological thriller. Detective Will Dormer, a veteran of the Los Angeles Police Department, temporarily escapes an internal affairs investigation that may ruin his career by travelling to Nightmute, Alaska, to investigate the murder of a 17-year-old girl. Evidence mounts up against a reclusive novelist, but the perpetual daylight of the summer months within the Arctic Circle wreaks sleep-depriving havoc on the detective's body clock and threatens his judgement.

programme.title: Morningside
programme.desc: In the run-down Toronto district of Scarborough, the lives of assorted residents are linked by the Morningside Community Centre, closing to be replaced by condominiums as part of encroaching gentrification. Personal struggles, relationships, jealousies and societal challenges are explored in a place where senseless gun violence is also on the rise. Tragedy is inevitable.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For decades, Dubai has sold itself as a safe place to live, do business and party. But when war broke out in nearby Iran, the city’s carefully curated image was shattered. Missiles and drones hit luxury hotels and critical infrastructure, expats scrambled for safety, and the government clamped down on sharing images of the war. Panorama reveals through the eyes of British residents, business owners and experts how Dubai’s promise of security and prosperity has been tested like never before.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie’s halfway through her college course and growing in confidence. On placement, she meets home carer Gary, who looks after his partner, Lisa.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piripenguins vow to change shopping-mad Brinicle’s wasteful ways. But when their clever recycling plan proves too exhausting, Looph comes up with an alternative solution.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard and Cosima undertake an exciting night-time mission rehoming a friendly slug that's taken residence in Cosima's bedroom!

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy thinks he's ace at skateboarding and decides to teach his daughters and the eaglets some tricks. Weasy quickly realises that one of the eaglets has much more talent than he does. Not wanting to lose his authority, Weasy decides to cheat.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The forest has run out of bath sponges and is becoming very stinky. Their latest order never arrived, and so the heroes must quest under the sea to Merhaven to collect them. However, Hoofius, whose singing stinks worse than his armpits, decides to enter the annual Merhaven Song Contest and accidentally causes a simple quest to collect bath sponges to turn into something much more perilous.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings decide to play hide-and-seek in the savannah.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck accidentally gets a job as the band conductor at the local park, Frog is keen to play trumpet in the band - but Duck is even more keen to stop him!

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Searching for traces of Laquium, Liko and Pagogo encounter a familiar Pokémon.

programme.title: Winx Club - The Magic Is Back
programme.desc: The Winx face their toughest physical exam yet, pushing their magic and teamwork to the limits. Meanwhile, Bloom attempts to bond with the mysterious Damien.

programme.title: The Next Step: Cheer
programme.desc: When a legendary coach abruptly leaves a highly successful cheerleading gym, the team are left in disarray, The Next Step’s Kenzie steps in to take over what’s left of the team, desperate to prove she is more than just a dancer, and that she can build this rag-tag group of athletes into a contender for nationals.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Wildlife superstar Robert Irwin puts his skills to the test with a photography challenge. We check out the latest read in the Blue Peter Book Club and find out how to make some tasty football biscuits!

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: After the scorching heat of early summer, Monty is working to keep the garden looking its best through the season's challenges. He tackles essential jobs, including cutting back perennials to make room for more colour, tending long grass and pruning step-over apples, while proving there's still time to sow vegetables for delicious late-summer harvests. Best of all, he enjoys some of summer's greatest rewards – lifting the first new potatoes and gathering fragrant sweet peas for the house. Adam Frost discovers a remarkable 2.5-acre walled garden in Surrey packed with hidden gems and unexpected delights. Sue Kent demonstrates how imagination and resourcefulness can transform everyday items into beautiful and productive growing spaces, while in Cornwall, there's a fruit and vegetable professor whose lifelong fascination with pests and diseases is helping gardeners grow healthier crops. Plus a visit to a grower with a striking collection of crassulas, showcasing the extraordinary beauty and diversity of these popular succulents.

programme.title: This Wild Life
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Saba Douglas-Hamilton, together with her husband Frank and their three young children, move to the romantic Samburu National Reserve in Kenya to run a safari camp for the first time. But life out there isn't easy. There are monkeys in the kitchen, floods threatening the camp and, with no fences to keep out the wildlife, elephants are regular house guests. This series follows their ups and downs as the family struggles to keep guests, elephants, lions and even crocodiles smiling. The family's first few weeks running a safari camp in a rugged outpost on northern Kenya's frontier have been a rollercoaster ride. Now the real test begins. The rainy season hits Samburu National Reserve. As a rogue flash flood strikes, Saba races to protect the camp and the animals. In the reserve, a mother elephant and her young calf are in trouble.

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
programme.desc: Screen adaptation of Oscar Wilde's celebrated play in which two Victorian gentlemen both adopt a double identity under the name of Ernest and end up in trouble when they both turn up at the same country house one weekend.

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The Great British Sewing Bee returns with brand new host Sophie Willan. Over ten weeks, 12 of the country’s best home sewers compete to be crowned winner of the Sewing Bee crown. In the opening week, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young set three challenges on the theme of the British summer. First up, the Pattern Challenge sees them crafting summer shorts with a patch pocket. Next, in the 90-minute Transformation Challenge, barbecue aprons become bags. Finally, there is a Sewing Bee first in the Made-to-Measure challenge as the sewers are asked to create garments to fit themselves. They become their own models as they make outfits for their ideal summer’s day, and once complete, the catwalk is all theirs. One sewer will impress and earn Garment of the Week, but for another, it’s the end of the road as they leave the sewing room for good.

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary heads to Edinburgh. Her late mother was Scottish, so the city holds a very special place in her heart. Edinburgh is full of excitement as it gears up for the marathon. It’s the second-largest marathon in the UK, with over 35,000 participants, and Mary has been given the honour of starting it. Mary meets runners and their families before the race and offers support along the way before heading to the finish line, where she's be joined by her granddaughter Grace. Together, they share Mary's delicious homemade chocolate rocky road with the hungry runners after their epic race. It’s an emotional and exhausting run for many, and Mary hopes her delicious treats will help put a spring back in their step. Later, she heads to the Royal Botanic Gardens to meet children from a local school as they work on the plots. She shares a recipe for pea pesto spaghetti inspired by the Gardens' produce.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui is property-hunting in Norwich and Wymondham in Norfolk. Meanwhile, Martin meets an entrepreneur from Berkshire who is working on a development at the top of the Rhondda Valley in Blaenrhondda. One property is destined for the rental market, another for resale, while a third is being transformed into the buyer's home.

programme.title: Jesus Christ Superstar
programme.desc: A novel take on the last seven days of Jesus Christ's life, based on Tim Rice and Andrew Lloyd Webber's famous rock opera. A group of young people arrive in the Holy Land by bus to act out events leading to the Crucifixion in a blend of biblical and contemporary themes. To Judas Iscariot, Christ is a badly managed superstar, bound to lose the support of a fickle public. To the priests, he is a menace, and to Mary Magdalene, he brings a love she has never known before. Photographed against dramatic scenery in Israel, the film is performed without dialogue and includes the hit songs I Don't Know How to Love Him, Superstar and King Herod's Song.

programme.title: Calamity Jane
programme.desc: Calamity Jane is the roughest, toughest gal in the town of Deadwood, and only 'Wild Bill' Hickok is man enough to discover the lady underneath the tough talk and gun belts. Doris Day and Howard Keel star in this musical based on the lives of Calamity Jane and 'Wild Bill' Hickok, two real celebrities of the American West.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This programme comes from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where hundreds of people are welcomed with their antiques and collectibles. The experts on hand are on the hunt for items of interest which will do well at auction, while Paul Martin finds out about the history of the location.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Classic wartime sitcom featuring the Home Guard of Walmington-on-Sea. Mainwaring and Wilson are in the bank when an unexpected guest drops in.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham embarks on an extraordinary journey through deep time to uncover how a creature as vast, complex and charismatic as the elephant came to be. From a tiny cell over four billion years ago to one of the planet’s most iconic giants, this is the surprising story of life's most radical transformation.

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Highlights from the third round as the best golfers in the world compete for the 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. Previous winners at the famous Southport links include Arnold Palmer, Tom Watson, Padraig Harrington and Jordan Spieth, who secured a dramatic victory when the Claret Jug was last won here back in 2017. World number one Scottie Scheffler started the week as defending champion following his emphatic triumph at Royal Portrush in 2025, with world number two Rory McIlroy hoping to bookend his major season with a Claret Jug to add to the Green Jacket he won at the Masters in April.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Relive some of the biggest hits of 2001 with these Top of the Pops classic performances. Kylie’s comeback was complete with a cyber-cool tune that shot to the top of the chart, Gabrielle sang the unrequited love song of the noughties, new boyband Blue got us on our feet, and Sophie Ellis-Bextor brought the house down with an anthem for the ages. Featuring Kylie Minogue, Destiny’s Child, U2, Shaggy and RikRok, Atomic Kitten, Gabrielle, The Dandy Warhols, Westlife, Wheatus, S Club, Nelly Furtado, Missy Elliott, Stereophonics, Blue, REM and Sophie Ellis-Bextor.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: As the new millennium begins, Top of the Pops is bigger, brighter and faster. Guitar bands bring their A-game to the show, from mosh pit megastars Feeder to Wheatus’s frat-rock jives and The Dandy Warhols’ bohemian vibes. Shaggy returns with RikRok with the best-selling single of the year – and an anthem for flat denial. ITV’s new talent show produces Hear’Say, Gorillaz graces the studio in virtual form, and the show witnesses the dawn of grime when So Solid Crew come to collect their crown. The new boyband of the year are Blue, and at the end of the year, Robbie and Nicole Kidman take the Christmas number one - but it's a studio singalong with Jocelyn Brown that rounds the year off with the heartfelt song of hope War Is Over. Contributors include Grant Nicholas and Taka Hirose of Feeder, Shaggy, Ricky ‘RikRok’ Ducent, Lisa Maffia of So Solid Crew, Brendan B Brown of Wheatus, Simon Webbe, Anthony Costa, Lee Ryan and Duncan James of Blue, and Courtney Taylor-Taylor and Zia McCabe of The Dandy Warhols.

programme.title: Kylie at the BBC
programme.desc: A journey back in time for a selection of Kylie’s best and biggest performances on the BBC. It’s a journey from the early, girl-next-door days with Stock Aitken Waterman to the boundary-pushing dance sounds of the 90s, 00s and beyond. Along for the ride are some of the eclectic mix of stars Kylie has collaborated with over the years, including Robbie Williams, Nick Cave and Jason Donovan. Together, this collection of magical BBC moments highlights why Kylie has always had a special place in the British public’s hearts.

programme.title: Madonna at the BBC
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of magical Madonna moments on various BBC shows, from her first Top of the Pops appearance with Holiday in 1984 right up to the present day. This collection covers Madonna's journey from Material Girl to Queen of Pop and captures how over the years her ability to combine charisma, controversy and classic pop tunes has made her the female icon of her times and a true global superstar.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Ruth Madeley unearths a long-buried family secret about an adoption, and a mystery that requires DNA testing and meticulous genealogical detective work to uncover a lost ancestor.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes an alphabetical journey through the world of gardening, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular garden presenters and programmes. In this episode, she's exploring subjects that all begin with the letter P. Programmes include: Gardeners' World, The One Show, RHS Chelsea Flower Show, Grow Your Own Veg and Flora Britannica. With presenters: Alys Fowler, Mike Dilger, Rachel de Thame, Christine Walkden, Monty Don, Laurence Llewellyn-Bowen and Carol Klein.

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden is in the air above two glorious Gloucestershire gardens. At Hidcote Manor Garden she cuts an underperforming shrub down to size and meets Sue, for whom the garden is an inspiration. At Kiftsgate Court Gardens she tackles an enormous rambling rose. We discover the arts and crafts heritage of the Cotswolds.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The next stop on Anita's road trip is West Mersea in Essex. While the county may be known for engineering and car manufacturing, along its coastline one industry has a much longer history. Oysters have been harvested here for more than 2,000 years, cultivated in the creeks around Mersea Island. Over the decades, oyster farming has adapted to changing environmental conditions and the arrival of the non-native Pacific rock oyster. To experience what oyster harvesting is like today, Anita heads onto the brackish waters to meet the people farming oysters. She also meets the team investigating how the non-native Pacific rock oyster is affecting the local ecosystem and whether it could play a role in helping coastal habitats respond to rising sea levels. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the people and projects shaping life around our coastline.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek goes island hopping, visiting Caldey off the Pembrokeshire coast, then Anglesey, known for its seabirds, before finishing in south Wales at Barry Island, his childhood home.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: The weather in this year’s growing season is making gardening a real challenge at Beechgrove, with cold grey skies and periods of high temperature giving Ruth Vichos and Brian Cunningham plenty to deal with. Ruth is in her vertical garden, where she has strawberries, blossoms and various climbing varieties all demonstrating what you can produce if you are short of space, using stacking systems and plants that will follow vertical surfaces. Brian reviews the wildflower beds he sowed in April, which will become a great resource for pollinators and the rest of the garden’s ecosystem. Brian and Ruth look at the diseases and pests that might be attacking your garden favourites right now and what to do about them. There is a visit to a great garden in East Lothian, and all the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt celebrates the best of seasonal produce with the help of some favourite Saturday Kitchen chefs. Bryn Williams puts courgettes centre stage by dressing them in a sharp and zesty citrus dressing and topping with a fillet of pan-fried sea bream. Meanwhile, Poppy O’Toole has three viral recipes – hot honey chicken, feta and pomegranate chopped salad and crispy smashed potato salad. Gennaro Contaldo tampers with an Italian classic, Ravneet Gill has a showstopping mango roulade with a creamy panna cotta filling, and Carmen O’Neal has a rundown of the best rums from around the world. Finally Vanessa Williams tucks into either her food heaven, butterflied bass with fries, or her food hell, pork and black bean tacos. Meanwhile, Rick Stein embarks on a moonlit fishing trip, and the Hairy Bikers hope to catch tuna in Sardinia.

programme.title: A Man for All Seasons
programme.desc: Multi-award-winning historical drama about Sir Thomas More's refusal to acknowledge Henry VIII's break with the Roman Catholic Church. King Henry VIII wants the pope to grant him a divorce from his barren wife to marry Anne Boleyn in order to get a male heir. Above all, he wants the endorsement of his newly appointed chancellor and widely respected scholar Sir Thomas More. But More has a divine conscience and will not consent to a break with the church of Rome. The scene is set for a struggle between the power of a king and a man who loves God more than his life. Paul Scofield's performance won one of the film's six Oscars, which also included the best picture award. Adapted by Robert Bolt from his own stage play.

programme.title: I Was Monty's Double
programme.desc: World War II drama. A low-ranking officer who resembles General Montgomery is recruited to play his double in an elaborate plan to trick the Nazis.

programme.title: GAA Live
programme.desc: Holly Hamilton presents live coverage of the All-Ireland Hurling final between Galway and Limerick from a sold-out Croke Park. It's one of the standout days in the Irish sporting calendar and is steeped in history, pride and fierce rivalry. Holly is joined in the studio by All-Ireland winners Paul Murphy and Séamus Flanagan, and Neil McManus. Mark Sidebottom provides pitch-side updates, with commentary by Thomas Niblock and Clare legend Jamesie O’Connor. Stars from the worlds of sport and entertainment are set to join the coverage for a special day in Irish sport.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the Flog It! team are in Greater Manchester, where over 800 people come along to have their items valued at Stockport Town Hall. Paul is joined by experts Mark Stacey and Philip Serrell as they pick out their favourite antiques to take to auction. Mark is wowed by a William De Morgan ceramic tile, Paul finds some etchings by artist John Buckland Wright and Philip discovers a gold necklace that surprises everyone at auction.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona and Philip unravel the mystery of a suspected Fabergé flower, kept hidden in a shoebox in Liverpool but believed to have belonged to royalty – and worth a small fortune!

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow comes from the beautiful grounds of the imposing Elizabethan mansion Wollaton Hall in Nottingham. Silver expert Kate Flitcroft is shaken, not stirred, by a stunning fire extinguisher cocktail shaker that turns up, while ceramics expert Eric Knowles meets his match with a cheeky toy monkey popping out of a musical pineapple. Fiona Bruce arrives in style on an iconic Chopper bike and tells the story of Raleigh Bicycles, founded in Nottingham in the 1880s. She learns about local activist George Powe, who campaigned to secure employment opportunities for black workers at Raleigh in the 1950s, and hears from two former employees about life inside what was once the world’s largest bicycle factory. Jewellery expert Susan Rumfitt challenges Fiona to guess the value of three intriguing cameo items, and collectables expert Hilary Kay is surprised to discover some of Queen Victoria’s personal undergarments with a sizeable price tag to boot.

programme.title: The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society
programme.desc: In London, 1946, author Juliet Ashton's career has taken off, but she is not enjoying promoting her detective stories to eager audiences on a book tour. When she receives a letter from a Guernsey pig farmer inviting her to attend a meeting of his literary society, she eagerly accepts. What Juliet discovers about her new friends and their secrets leads to her staying on the island much longer than she planned.

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Highlights from the final day of the 154th Open as the Champion Golfer of the Year is crowned at Royal Birkdale. Previous winners at the famous Southport links include Arnold Palmer, Tom Watson, Padraig Harrington and Jordan Spieth, who secured a dramatic victory when the Claret Jug was last won here back in 2017. World number one Scottie Scheffler started the week as defending champion following his emphatic triumph at Royal Portrush in 2025, with world number two Rory McIlroy hoping to bookend his major season with a Claret Jug to add to the Green Jacket he won at the Masters in April.
